channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: This AI-powered shape-shifting wing could make aircraft tails obsolete — and slash travel costs
item.description: The German Aerospace Center is developing a 1 million Euro project exploring the use of morphing wings, redefining how we think of airplanes.

item.title: Un nouveau lien mystérieux entre la théorie de la chaleur et les trous noirs !
item.description: Il y a environ 50 ans, Stephen Hawking et son collègue Jacob Bekenstein établissaient un lien profond entre la thermodynamique, qui gouverne l'Univers des étoiles aux réactions chimiques de la vie, et les trous noirs, ces manifestations spectaculaires de la courbure de l'espace-temps d'Einstein....

item.title: States make last-ditch effort to stop the Paramount ‘media behemoth’
item.description: A dozen state attorneys general are trying to block the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery they warn would raise movie prices and crush cable TV distributors. The states - California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington - filed suit on Monday, arguing […]...

item.title: iCloud+ vs. Apple One: Which is worth it for you?
item.description: The two subscription services offer more than just extra storage, but getting the right one can save you money.

item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

item.title: ‘The fate of humanity must not be decided behind closed doors’: US artificial intelligence sovereign wealth fund sees surge in support as AI job losses mount — 69% of Americans want to see half of AI stock placed into new state-owned investment fund
item.description: There is now overwhelming support in the US for AI firms to contribute to a sovereign wealth fund designed to redistribute wealth to working class Americans.

item.title: Compteur, GPS, alarme : on a vu Velone, le boîtier vélo avec 1 an d’autonomie
item.description: Concentré de fonctionnalités pour augmenter l’expérience à vélo, le boîtier Velone fait autant office de compteur que d’alarme ou de GPS, avec une grosse autonomie. On l'a vu, voici notre premier avis.

item.title: Wi-Fi 8 Explained: Features, Release Date, and More
item.description: Chipset makers and router manufacturers are talking about Wi-Fi 8, but what is the new standard, and when will it arrive?

item.title: The EV market is on the road to recovery thanks to high gas prices
item.description: As the US war on Iran sent gas prices soaring, American car buyers flocked to electric vehicles - and in particular hybrids. After a sluggish winter and fall, EV sales rebounded in the second quarter of 2026, with automakers posting some of their best numbers since the elimination of the federal EV tax credit last […]

item.title: Taycan, Jetta, ID.5 : la liste noire des 10 modèles menacés chez le groupe Volkswagen
item.description: Une dizaine de modèles Volkswagen, Audi et Porsche figureraient sur une liste noire relayée par la presse allemande. Rien d'officiel, mais la Taycan et la Jetta y seraient, et le contexte donne du crédit à la fuite.

item.title: TV-logging app TV Time is functionally relaunching as parent company pivots to AI
item.description: Bingers is expected to launch by the end of July as a replacement for TV Time.

item.title: No room in your kitchen for a Ninja Slushi? We tested 3 blenders that make perfect slushies for one for a fraction of the price, and actually fit in your cupboard
item.description: If you don't have the space or cash for a Ninja Slushi machine, these portable blenders can make fantastic frozen drinks for a fraction of the price.

item.title: Gen Z is ‘job scrolling’ while on the clock, in the office. Here’s what that says about the workplace
item.description: Hiring may have slowed down during the summer months, but that doesn’t mean Gen Z’s job search has, too. As job creation and hiring continues to slow down during the summer months—the economy added only 57,000 new jobs in June—a new workplace trend called “job scrolling” is emerging.

item.title: Why is everyone eating Cream of Wheat on TikTok?
item.description: Cream of Wheat is viral on TikTok as grocery prices stay high and shoppers turn to affordable, nostalgic comfort foods.

item.title: Hands-on with Tiny Vinyls new tiny record player
item.description: Tiny Vinyl has released a tiny record player, crate, and display frame for its miniature singles. They're available at Target.

item.title: Memory chip stocks down again today: Why SK Hynix shares are tumbling after its record-breaking IPO
item.description: SKHY fell 9% Monday, just days after its blockbuster public debut. Micron and Sandisk are also seeing a slump amid a semiconductor sell-off. Shares in memory chip companies slumped across U.S. markets Monday, in a further sign of investor concerns over how much road is left for the AI stock market boom.

item.title: 'The precision and quality of the print finish are exceptional': We love the beginner-friendly Anycubic Kobra S1 Combo 3D printer — and it's got a massive discount right now
item.description: The high-speed, beginner-friendly Anycubic Kobra S1 Combo is one of the best 3D printers we've ever tested.

item.title: How Tom Holland’s nonalcoholic beer brand is riding the sober wave
item.description: Bero is positioning itself as the go-to for social drinkers who skip the booze—from Target aisles to World Cup watch parties. At a restaurant in lower Manhattan in mid-June, actor Tom Holland holds court with influencers and publicists. But the sheer number of beverage industry insiders means that he isn’t here to promote his upcoming return to the big screen as Spiderman. He’s talking up Bero—the nonalcholic beer brand he started in 2024, two years after the actor’s successful dry January sparked an interest in a sober lifestyle....

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."
